subject: Changes for running has-api under TLS/https
commit_message:
Changes for running has-api under TLS/https

Changes for running has-api under TLS/https on uwsgi/nginx - See optf/has project's Dockerfile for chagnes on how nginx is used. The 'conductor' file here basically is an nginx configuration file pointing to the cert and keys. The values.yaml received chagnes for the docker image version bumpup

Change-Id: I732262f41526b5857d4bffdd0a3fbb5fc4266dcd
Signed-off-by: Shankar Narayanan <snarayanan@research.att.com>
Issue-ID: OPTFRA-461
Signed-off-by: Shankar Narayanan <snarayanan@research.att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/1934/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/1934/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/1934

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Looks Good. Like the comments in the values.yaml say, this release will expect a new image tagged 2.0.0. But ofcourse, anyone can use any of their own values files to point to any dockercentral of their choice (that allows the image pull of course) to get the required image.

Also - please see the latest review of the oof/has project (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/85004/) for what changes are expected in the new image and how resources from helm charts in this review will map into locations inside that image (for example, certs and nginx config file) - And how the nginx command runs the api in uwsgi container installed from the image.

Thanks
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.
